summary: Verified Google Play reviewer seed account end-to-end. All 7 pytest cases passed: (1) POST /api/auth/login with googleplay.reviewer@quickgig.app / GooglePlay2026! returns 200 with valid 3-part JWT (263 chars) and user payload; (2) GET /api/auth/me with token returns the reviewer profile (name='Google Play Reviewer', is_verified=true, role='user', banned=false) and does NOT leak _id, password, or password_hash; (3) After `sudo supervisorctl restart backend`, login still succeeds (seed_reviewer() runs on startup and restores the account if missing); (4) After 2 additional restarts, direct MongoDB query on quickgig_database.users confirms EXACTLY ONE document with email==googleplay.reviewer@quickgig.app (idempotent seed — no duplicates); (5) Wrong password returns 401 and wrong email returns 401 (password is bcrypt-hashed and enforced). Credentials in /app/memory/test_credentials.md match the seed constants in server.py (REVIEWER_EMAIL/REVIEWER_PASSWORD env override supported).
critical_code_review_comments: seed_reviewer() (server.py:2074-2105) correctly uses find_one before insert — idempotent by design and does NOT overwrite existing doc, matching the /app/auth_testing.md guideline that seeds must be idempotent.
Reviewer password is stored as password_hash (bcrypt via hash_password helper), never plain text; verified by the wrong-password 401 test.
Email is lowercased before insert (server.py:2083) matching the login flow (server.py:363) which also lowercases input — no case-mismatch risk.

REVIEWER_EMAIL / REVIEWER_PASSWORD env overrides are supported but not documented in /app/backend/.env; not a bug — the hardcoded defaults are the ones referenced in Google Play submission and /app/memory/test_credentials.md.
on_startup() awaits seed_reviewer() after seed_admin() — startup path is clean; unique index on users.email (line 2111) further guarantees no duplicates even under a race.
public_user() correctly excludes _id and password_hash — verified via test_me_returns_reviewer_profile which asserts absence of those keys.

Minor cosmetic drift: existing DB doc has bio='Test account for Google Play Console review.' while current code says 'Test account for Google Play Console / App Store review.' — expected because seed is idempotent (never overwrites). Not a bug; if bio wording matters, main agent could add a one-time update on startup, but current behavior is what was requested (never overwrite).
seed_data_creation: No new seed data created. Reviewer account is auto-seeded by backend startup via seed_reviewer(). Test does not mutate the account; only reads and restarts the backend.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Reviewer account tests live at /app/backend/tests/test_reviewer_account.py — 7 tests covering login success, wrong password/email 401s, /api/auth/me, restart persistence, and Mongo duplicate check. Run: `cd /app/backend && pytest tests/test_reviewer_account.py -v`. Tests use supervisorctl to restart backend so they must run with sudo access (already available in this env). Credentials: googleplay.reviewer@quickgig.app / GooglePlay2026! (documented in /app/memory/test_credentials.md — DO NOT change).
rca_of_issue: No bugs. Feature works as specified: reviewer account is idempotently seeded on backend startup, login/JWT/me flow returns correct payload, wrong password is rejected, and repeated restarts do not create duplicate users.
